ec2_instance.description: Ensure all data stored in the Instance is securely encrypted. Checks that root_block_device has encrypted = true on aws_instance resources generated by the LLM.

ebs.description: Ensure EBS volumes are encrypted at rest. Any EBS resource regenerated during a DOWNSIZE or ghost cleanup action must be encrypted — unencrypted storage is a security violation regardless of remediation context.
ebs.explanation: Downsizing an EX2 instance can involve stopping it and recreating its storage (which needs to be encrypted)

s3_infrastructure.description: Ensure S3 buckets have server-side encryption enabled. Applies to the Terraform remote state bucket and the tfstate snapshot bucket. Terraform state contains resource ARNs, instance IDs, and sometimes credentials.
Ensure S3 buckets block all public ACLs. Applies to the remote state bucket and snapshot bucket. State must never be publicly accessible.

Ensure the S3 state bucket has access logging enabled. The audit trail depends on knowing who accessed or modified Terraform state. Note: customer-owned buckets are not managed in Terraform and are excluded.

cloudwatch_log_groups.description: Ensure CloudWatch log groups have a retention period of at least 365 days. Validates that Agent 3 actually set a compliant retention value when fixing a SET_RETENTION finding — guards against generating retention_in_days = 7.

iam.description: Ensure IAM policies do not use wildcard actions. Agent 4 must have minimum-privilege — only specific actions like ec2:StopInstances or ec2:ModifyInstanceAttribute are allowed, never ec2:*.
Ensure IAM policies are attached to groups or roles only, never directly to users. Agent 4 operates via an IAM role — a direct user attachment indicates an incorrect Terraform generation.
Ensure the AdministratorAccess managed policy is not attached to any role. Must never appear in any Agent 4 role. Hard security boundary for the execution agent.

ec2.description: EC2 termination protection. Suppressed globally because TERMINATE is a valid remediation action — termination protection must be absent on instances flagged for deletion.

s3_infrastructure.description: S3 bucket access logging. Suppressed because customer-owned buckets are not managed in Terraform — only the two platform infrastructure buckets are. Access logging on customer buckets is out of scope.
S3 cross-region replication. Suppressed — out of scope for this project entirely. No multi-region requirements exist within the 2.5-month timeline and free tier budget.
S3 event notifications. Suppressed — out of scope. No event-driven S3 pipeline exists in the current architecture.
S3 bucket uses KMS CMK for encryption. Suppressed due to free tier budget constraint — KMS CMK incurs per-request costs. SSE-S3 enforced by CKV_AWS_19 is the accepted alternative.
